title: Writing - Part 1: Sports and Competition
description: Your class has been discussing the role of competitive sport in school.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the aspects in your notes. You should explain which aspect you consider most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

aspects: Encouraging a healthy and active lifestyle
opinions: Competition teaches valuable lessons about winning and losing.
Schools put too much emphasis on winning and not enough on participation.
Sport is one of the best ways to keep young people active and healthy.

structure.introduction: Introduce school sport and competition and the two aspects you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first aspect (e.g., building teamwork) and its social benefits.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second aspect (e.g., encouraging a healthy lifestyle) and its importance for young people.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both aspects and state which you consider most important.

content.modelAnswer:
Competitive sport has long been a central part of school life in many countries. While it brings clear benefits, some argue that the emphasis on competition can be damaging for certain students. This essay will discuss how sport builds teamwork and how it promotes a healthy and active lifestyle among young people.

Team sports in particular teach young people how to work effectively with others towards a common goal. Players must communicate, trust their teammates and put the group's success above personal glory. These skills are not only valuable on the sports field but are highly transferable to the workplace and other social situations.

Equally important is the role sport plays in encouraging young people to stay active. With childhood obesity becoming an increasing concern, schools have a responsibility to promote regular physical activity. Competitive sport gives students a motivating reason to exercise and can instil habits of fitness that last throughout their lives.

In conclusion, while teamwork is a valuable outcome of competitive sport, I believe its contribution to a healthy lifestyle is the most significant benefit. The global rise in sedentary behaviour and related health problems makes encouraging physical activity in schools more urgent than ever. Sport is one of the most enjoyable and effective ways to address this challenge.
